name: Barton Golf Club
about: Barton Golf Club is a family-owned and operated golf facility located in the Northeast Kingdom of Vermont. The venue features both an outdoor 18-hole course and indoor golf simulator capabilities. The atmosphere is described as casual and friendly, with scenic views of the surrounding region. The indoor golf area is available for booking by the hour, and the club hosts various tournaments and leagues throughout the year.
why_golfers_like_it: Variety of booking options for indoor golf by the hour
Hosts tournaments and leagues for all skill levels
Affordable pricing for greens fees and simulator time
Scenic location in the Vermont Northeast Kingdom

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of food service on-site
No mention of alcohol service on-site

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify club rental policies for the indoor simulator.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: There is no mention of food and drink policies on the website.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: 1 hour is typically the time it takes for one player to play 18 holes or 2 players to play 9 holes.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is described as family-owned and operated with a casual atmosphere, making it suitable for families and juniors.
